opinion.h4: WORTHINGTON CORP. v. MLODOZENIEC ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE COURT OF APPEALS OF NEW YORK. No. 515. Decided January 9, 1961.
opinion.p: Reported below: See 9 App. Div. 2d 21, 189 N.Y.S.2d 468.
Louis J. Lefkowitz, Attorney General of New York, Paxton Blair, Solicitor General, and Roy Wiedersum and Gilbert M. Landy, Assistant Attorneys General, for appellees.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
opinion.h4: COUNTY OF LAWRENCE v. UNITED STATES, 364 U.S. 628 (1961)
opinion.p: 364 U.S. 628 COUNTY OF LAWRENCE ET AL. v. UNITED STATES. APPEAL FROM THE UNITED STATES COURT OF APPEALS FOR THE THIRD CIRCUIT. No. 528. Decided January 9, 1961.
Robert C. Lea, Jr. for appellants.
Solicitor General Rankin, Assistant Attorney General Morton and Roger P. Marquis for the United States.
The motion to affirm is granted and the judgment is affirmed.
